mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered instances over a 5-year walk-forward test, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and real GDP confirms robust aggregate demand, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and GDPC1 yields negative mean forward returns with statistical significance (p < 0.05) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month acceleration in industrial production indicates immediate supply-side capacity utilization and inventory restocking, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of instances when INDPRO accelerates during recovery phases, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 2.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in > 70% of high-inflation months without subsequent rate hikes, or t-statistic < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window, or forward returns are negative in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect increased construction activity and durable goods demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases consistently fail to predict positive 20-day returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth combined with elevated inflation creates a stagflationary supply bottleneck that forces monetary tightening and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of occurrences during late-cycle regimes, or Rank IC remains negative.
